signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical Escalation + Carry + Intervention Risk
signals.text: USD/JPY at 162.34 — 275bp Fed-BoJ carry sustains the bid despite US-Iran hostilities. JPY COT LF net short -105,238 (crowded carry) remains vulnerable to a sharp unwind if VIX exceeds 25, though current VIX at 18.8 (normal volatility) keeps carry dominant. 160.00 remains the key level previously triggering BoJ warnings; intervention risk is acute on sustained trading above 162.00. Options market is pricing protection against a reversal with 25d RR at -1.37 (moderate put skew).

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: RBNZ 2.5%. With NZD CESI at neutral (net 0) and COT long% at an extreme low of 15.2%, a hawkish RBNZ delivery creates significant short-squeeze risk. NZD/USD at 0.5854 is supported by a -0.58% real rate, which is less accommodative than JPY (-0.52%). Retail sentiment is 85% short, diverging from the hawkish CB bias, signaling potential retail capitulation if price extends higher.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Volatility + CESI Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6991 — IV at 12.1% vs HV30 at 6.4% generates a +5.7% VRP, signaling elevated institutional hedging demand. AUD CESI at -13 (strong negative) confirms persistent data misses, weighing on the 60bp AUD carry advantage (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%). COT LF net long +21,946 (63.2% long) faces liquidation risk if data continues to disappoint. Watch for a break of 0.6950 to confirm a shift in the carry regime.

signals.title: USD/CAD — Correlation Break + Tariff Risk
signals.text: USD/CAD at 1.4007 — Correlation vs WTI Oil at -0.933 vs norm of -0.006 (z=-1.91σ) indicates a severe breakdown in the traditional CAD-energy link. CAD is outperforming (1W avg +0.232%) despite Trump's tariff threats related to wildfire smoke. Fed 3.75% vs BoC 2.25% (+150bp) carry favors USD, but CAD resilience suggests the market is discounting tariff headlines. Watch the next BoC meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) for a shift in the 132bp OIS carry spread.
